title: Bicornella tenerrima
intro: Bicornella tenerrima, also known as Wild Yam, is a perennial vine native to North America. The health benefits of Wild Yam include its use as a natural remedy for menstrual cramps, menopausal symptoms, and other gynecological issues due to its anti-inflammatory and estrogenic properties. The bioactive constituents of Wild Yam include diosgenin, a steroidal saponin with hormonal activity, as well as other steroidal compounds and alkaloids. In traditional herbal medicine, Wild Yam is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or dried root to alleviate symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome, menopause, and other conditions related to reproductive health.
taxonomy: Bicornella tenerrima is a species of fungus in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It belongs to the phylum Bryophyta, which includes mosses and liverworts. This fungus is classified as a bryophyte, a group of non-vascular plants that grow in moist environments. Bicornella tenerrima is further classified as a member of the class Marchantiopsida, within the division Marchantiophyta. The classification of Bicornella tenerrima reflects its unique characteristics and growth habits.
